title: Wall Street plunges in AI ‘bloodbath’
excerpt: The US stock market sank on Friday in a “bloodbath” for companies linked to the AI boom.
content_html:
The US stock market sank on Friday in a "bloodbath" for companies linked to the AI boom.

Shares on Wall Street fell heavily in the wake of sharp downturns in Japan and Taiwan as investors raised questions about the soaring valuations of companies tied to the technology.

Chip manufacturers were hit hardest after a blistering rally earlier in the year driven by surging demand.

The Nasdaq Composite, which is dominated by tech companies, plunged by 1.9pc in its sharpest fall in a month.

Kei Okamura, portfolio manager at financial adviser Neuberger Berman, said: "The word 'bloodbath' is accurate because it is across the board."

Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 sank 4pc on Friday, having declined more than 10pc since its record high on June 25.

Taiwan's Taiex index had shed 6.5pc after $2tn chip giant TSMC fell by 7.3pc a day after announcing record second-quarter profit but higher than expected spending plans.

Jim Reid, an analyst at Deutsche Bank, said: "Global equities are continuing to slump, as fresh doubts about the AI trade have driven a pronounced sell-off in tech stocks."

Mr Reid said there had been no single trigger for the downturn, which came as the world's biggest technology companies prepared to report their second quarter results.

Tech stocks in Hong Kong were hit hard by Chinese AI start-up Moonshot, which on Friday unveiled a new large language model able to perform close to the levels of tools from US giant Anthropic.

The Beijing-based company unveiled Kimi K3, which it described as the world's largest open AI model by parameter count, an indicator of its complexity.

Lian Jye Su, an industry analyst, said Chinese models were gaining traction because they could be deployed far more cheaply than leading US systems.

"They can be run at a fraction of the cost that OpenAI charges its clients," he said.

Markets have also come under pressure from the renewed hostilities in the Middle East, which have sent oil prices soaring more than 15pc this month to over $85 a barrel.

06:45pm

Signing off...

Thanks for following our updates on markets today as Wall Street was hit by sharp drops in technology companies on fears surrounding an AI bubble. 

Chip stocks suffered heavy drops on Friday after Beijing startup Moonshot unveiled its new highly sophisticated Kimi-K3 AI model. 

It led to concerns about the huge investments into AI infrastructure which have fuelled the AI boom, leading to sharp selling of technology stocks. 

06:40pm

FTSE 100 finishes higher as rising oil price boost index
content_text: Tech stocks are selling off for the second consecutive day on Wall Street - REUTERS/Brendan McDermid The US stock market sank on Friday in a "bloodbath" for companies linked to the AI boom. Shares on Wall Street fell heavily in the wake of sharp downturns in Japan and Taiwan as investors raised questions about the soaring valuations of companies tied to the technology. Chip manufacturers were hit hardest after a blistering rally earlier in the year driven by surging demand. The Nasdaq Composite, which is dominated by tech companies, plunged by 1.9pc in its sharpest fall in a month. Kei Okamura, portfolio manager at financial adviser Neuberger Berman, said: "The word 'bloodbath' is accurate because it is across the board." Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 sank 4pc on Friday, having declined more than 10pc since its record high on June 25. Taiwan's Taiex index had shed 6.5pc after $2tn chip giant TSMC fell by 7.3pc a day after announcing record second-quarter profit but higher than expected spending plans. Jim Reid, an analyst at Deutsche Bank, said: "Global equities are continuing to slump, as fresh doubts about the AI trade have driven a pronounced sell-off in tech stocks." Mr Reid said there had been no single trigger for the downturn, which came as the world's biggest technology companies prepared to report their second quarter results. Tech stocks in Hong Kong were hit hard by Chinese AI start-up Moonshot, which on Friday unveiled a new large language model able to perform close to the levels of tools from US giant Anthropic. The Beijing-based company unveiled Kimi K3, which it described as the world's largest open AI model by parameter count, an indicator of its complexity. Lian Jye Su, an industry analyst, said Chinese models were gaining traction because they could be deployed far more cheaply than leading US systems. "They can be run at a fraction of the cost that OpenAI charges its clients," he said. Markets have also come under pressure from the renewed hostilities in the Middle East, which have sent oil prices soaring more than 15pc this month to over $85 a barrel. 06:45pm Signing off... Thanks for following our updates on markets today as Wall Street was hit by sharp drops in technology companies on fears surrounding an AI bubble. Chip stocks suffered heavy drops on Friday after Beijing startup Moonshot unveiled its new highly sophisticated Kimi-K3 AI model. It led to concerns about the huge investments into AI infrastructure which have fuelled the AI boom, leading to sharp selling of technology stocks. 06:40pm FTSE 100 finishes higher as rising oil price boost index

sentences.en: FTSE 100 finishes higher as rising oil price boost index
sentences.zh: 油价上涨推动指数上涨，富时 100 指数收高

content_html_zh:
周五，美国股市暴跌，引发了与人工智能热潮相关的公司的“大屠杀”。

日本和台湾股市急剧下滑，投资者对与该技术相关的公司估值飙升提出质疑，华尔街股市大幅下跌。

芯片制造商在今年早些时候因需求激增而大幅上涨后，受到的打击最为严重。

以科技公司为主的纳斯达克综合指数暴跌 1.9%，创一个月来最大跌幅。

金融顾问 Neuberger Berman 的投资组合经理 Kei Okamura 表示：“‘大屠杀’这个词是准确的，因为它是全面的。”

日本基准日经 225 指数周五下跌 4%，自 6 月 25 日创下历史新高以来已下跌逾 10%。

价值 2 万亿美元的芯片巨头台积电宣布第二季度利润创历史新高，但支出计划高于预期，该公司股价一天下跌 7.3%，台湾股市指数下跌 6.5%。

德意志银行分析师吉姆·里德表示：“全球股市持续下跌，因为对人工智能行业的新疑虑导致科技股大幅抛售。”

里德表示，经济衰退并不是由单一因素引发的，当时全球最大的科技公司正准备公布第二季度业绩。

香港科技股受到中国人工智能初创公司 Moonshot 的重创，该公司周五推出了一种新的大型语言模型，其性能接近美国巨头 Anthropic 的工具水平。

这家总部位于北京的公司推出了 Kimi K3，将其描述为世界上最大的开放式人工智能模型（按参数数量计算），参数数量反映了其复杂性。

行业分析师 Lian Jye Su 表示，中国模式正在获得关注，因为它们的部署成本比美国领先系统低得多。

“它们的运行成本只是 OpenAI 向客户收取的费用的一小部分，”他说。

中东地区重新爆发的敌对行动也给市场带来压力，本月油价飙升逾 15%，至每桶 85 美元以上。

06:45pm

正在注销...

感谢您关注我们今天的市场更新，因为对人工智能泡沫的担忧，华尔街科技公司股价大幅下跌。

在北京初创公司Moonshot推出其高度复杂的新型Kimi-K3人工智能模型后，周五芯片股大幅下跌。

这引发了人们对人工智能基础设施巨额投资的担忧，这些投资推动了人工智能的繁荣，导致科技股大幅抛售。

06:40pm

油价上涨推动指数上涨，富时 100 指数收高
